name: Twin Volt
explanation: A fat dual-VCO monophonic synth voice in the classic late-70s mono lineage. Two detuned analog-style oscillators (a saw and a pulse) plus a square sub-oscillator an octave down are summed and ALSO ring-modulated against each other, so the Ring control blends clangy, inharmonic metallic colour into the tone. The mix drives a punchy 4-pole resonant low-pass with its own decay envelope, then an amp envelope; pitch glides between notes. Great for fat basses and clangy leads. Controls: Cutoff, Resonance, Env Amount, Ring, Detune, Decay and Level.
